sections.content:
RIGHT didn't work at c26-30. Player is still at r14-18, c26-30. The player is blocked from going right. Looking at the surroundings:
r14: WBbbbbbBWWWWWWWBRBWWW
The player is at columns 1-5 (c26-30). To the right is a wall at column 6 (c31), then white area at columns 7-13 (c32-38).
So the player is blocked by a wall at c31. The player can't go RIGHT.
Let me check what other directions are available. The player is at r14-18, c26-30. Let me try DOWN to see if there's a path below.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player at r14-18, c26-30. RIGHT blocked by wall at c31. White area visible to the right but unreachable. Need to try DOWN to continue.
Goal model: Navigate to goal at r49-55, c43-49.
Action model: Player moves 6 cells per step. Try DOWN to find path.
Plan: Try DOWN to see if there's a vertical path from here.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try DOWN
result = action(['DOWN'])
print(f"After DOWN: done={last_action_result.get('done')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for node in nodes:
    if node['color'] == 'b' and node['pixels'] == 24:
        rows = [p[0] for p in node['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in node['boundary']]
        print(f"Player: r{min(rows)}-{max(rows)} c{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check surroundings
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
pr = [n for n in nodes if n['color']=='b' and n['pixels']==24][0]
pr_min, pr_max = min([p[0] for p in pr['boundary']]), max([p[0] for p in pr['boundary']])
pc_min, pc_max = min([p[1] for p in pr['boundary']]), max([p[1] for p in pr['boundary']])
print(f"\nAround player:")
for r in range(max(0,pr_min-2), min(64,pr_max+8)):
    print(f"r{r:2d}: {ascii_lines[r][pc_min-2:pc_max+15]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
